summary: INV-F5-V2 binds to advisor prompt-cache at lib/prompt-cache.ts:22-29 (cache-key parts: canonicalPrompt + sourceSignature + workspaceRoot + runtime + maxTokens + thresholdConfig). NO generation field at the type level. Two read-through invalidation call sites: skill-advisor-brief.ts:442 and advisor-recommend.ts:173. Rollback path at promotion/rollback.ts:36-48 accepts a generic invalidateCache callback BUT no production caller wires it to advisorPromptCache. The actually-load-bearing seam is the missing wire from rollbackPromotion()'s caller, not the swallowed-error path that F37 #7 targets

newContent: After F37 #7 surgical fix (return rolledBack:false on cacheInvalidationError), the upstream caller of rollbackPromotion() MUST pass advisorPromptCache.invalidateSourceSignatureChange(newSourceSignature) (or advisorPromptCache.clear() if signature derivation is deferred) as the invalidateCache callback. Currently no production caller wires this. Located: future iter 10 should grep callers of rollbackPromotion to identify the binding site.

summary: Iter-6 F31 RETRACTED. The scorer's graph_causal lane operates on a SKILL-graph (5 edge types: enhances, siblings, depends_on, prerequisite_for, conflicts_with) loaded from .opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/database/skill-graph.sqlite. It does NOT consume code-graph edges. Iter-1 F3's 5-class code-graph flattening cannot bias the scorer; the seam doesn't exist. F37 #9 blast radius downgraded from cross-system to subsystem

Iter-8 F44 'Day-1 = 5 lines' framing is misleading. Verified Day-1 edit cost is ~20 LOC across 2 files (parity test + settings JSON). Severity tier (P0) and effort tier (S) unchanged; framing should be updated to 'S (~20 LOC, 2 files)'

Hidden invariant: lib/prompt-cache.ts:40-43 derives the HMAC secret from process.pid + performance.timeOrigin + Math.random(), so the cache cannot survive process restart. INV-F5-V2 stale-serve window is bounded by (5min TTL × single host process × per-process secret rotation). F37 #7 closing benchmark must assert that within a single host process, post-rollback prompt-cache lookups don't serve pre-rollback briefs at the SAME canonicalPrompt+sourceSignature

reason: Type-level read of AdvisorProjection.edgeType (5-element skill-graph union) and projection.ts SKILL_GRAPH_DB constant prove the scorer reads skill-graph.sqlite, not code-graph.sqlite. No imports, no shared schema, no shared edge taxonomy between code-graph emit pipeline and skill-advisor scorer.
